column1: Etymological Evolution of Apollo: Chronological Table

column6: Orphic Hymns, Julian’s Hymn to Helios

column2: c. 1500–1200 BCE: Anatolian Apal'un → Hittite Appaliunaš (Wilusa/Troy)
c. 1350–1200 BCE: Mycenaean A-pa-li-u (Linear B tablets)
c. 800–600 BCE: Homeric Apóllōn (Panhellenic, Archaic Greece)
c. 600–323 BCE: Phoebus Apollo (Classical Greece, Delphi)
c. 323–30 BCE: Apollo Helios (Hellenistic solar syncretism)
c. 30 BCE–300 CE: Imperial Apollo (Roman Empire)
Dates are approximate and based on earliest or most significant attestations.
Syncretisms often overlap in time and region, especially in the Hellenistic and Roman periods.
Some forms (e.g., Apal'un, Appaliunaš) are reconstructed and not directly attested in Greek sources.
The Roman cult of Apollo Medicus is specifically dated to the founding of his temple in Rome after a plague in 433 BCE.
If you need a visual timeline or further expansion on any entry, let me know!
